title: Citrusy Crispy Peruvian Fish Agemono
description: A Peruvian dish featuring crispy deep-fried fish coated in a tangy citrusy batter, served with a side of zesty slaw.

ingredients.name: 1 pound firm white fish (such as halibut or snapper)

instructions: Cut the fish into 1-inch pieces and season with salt, black pepper, and cayenne pepper.
In a shallow dish, mix together the flour, tamarind paste, and lime juice.
Pour the club soda into the flour mixture and stir until just combined.
Add the fish pieces to the batter and toss to coat.
Heat about 1/2-inch of vegetable oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat until it reaches 350°F.
Fry the fish in batches until golden brown and crispy, about 2-3 minutes per piece.
Drain the fish on paper towels and serve hot with a side of zesty slaw.
To make the slaw, combine the shredded cabbage and carrots in a bowl and toss with a squeeze of lime juice and a pinch of salt.
Serve the fried fish on a plate with a dollop of slaw on top.
